headline.purpose: Screen-level task headings and selected dates.

title.purpose: Settings sections, project names, and panel headings.

body.purpose: Task names, form values, descriptions, and general UI copy.

label.purpose: View labels, metadata labels, and compact form labels.

shadows.name: low-row-lift
shadows.purpose: Separates task rows, inline composer, and settings cards.

shadows.name: dialog-lift
shadows.purpose: Lifts modal task creation above the app shell.

motion.name: panel-resize
motion.purpose: Sidebar and detail pane width transitions.

motion.name: state-color
motion.purpose: Hover, active, focus, and selected state changes.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Primary action button for adding tasks and saving important actions.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary{height:32px;display:inline-flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;gap:6px;border:1px solid transparent;border-radius:8px;background:var(--primary);color:var(--primary-foreground);padding:0 10px;font:500 14px/1.2 var(--font-sans);transition:background-color .18s ease,color .18s ease,transform .12s ease,box-shadow .18s ease}.ds-btn-primary:hover{background:color-mix(in oklch,var(--primary),black 8%)}.ds-btn-primary:active{transform:translateY(1px)}.ds-btn-primary:focus-visible{outline:2px solid var(--ring);outline-offset:2px}.ds-btn-primary svg{width:16px;height:16px;stroke:currentColor;stroke-width:2;fill:none;stroke-linecap:round}

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Secondary command button for choosing folders, toggling options, and non-primary actions.

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Compact task, project, date, and folder field.

components.name: Task Row
components.description: The canonical repeated task item with completion state, priority dot, date metadata, and project chip.
components.html:
Finish release notes
2026-05-29 · 09:00 · Project
components.css: .ds-task-row{display:grid;grid-template-columns:32px minmax(0,1fr);align-items:center;gap:12px;border:1px solid var(--border);border-radius:8px;background:color-mix(in oklch,var(--card),transparent 20%);padding:8px 12px;box-shadow:0 1px 2px rgb(0 0 0 / .05);color:var(--foreground);transition:border-color .18s ease,background-color .18s ease}.ds-task-row:hover{border-color:color-mix(in oklch,var(--ring),transparent 30%)}.ds-check{width:24px;height:24px;border-radius:999px;border:1px solid var(--input);background:var(--background)}.ds-task-main{min-width:0}.ds-task-title{display:flex;align-items:center;gap:8px;font:500 14px/1.25 var(--font-sans)}.ds-dot{width:8px;height:8px;border-radius:999px;background:rgb(245 158 11)}.ds-task-meta{margin-top:4px;color:var(--muted-foreground);font:400 12px/1.3 var(--font-sans)}.ds-chip{display:inline-flex;border:1px solid var(--border);border-radius:999px;padding:2px 8px}

components.name: Sidebar Navigation Item
components.description: Collapsed or expanded side rail navigation command.

narrative.overview: DDL Todo should feel like a compact local command center for deadline work. The surface is quiet, lightly tinted, and precise: users should see dates, task status, project context, reminder state, and folder affordances before they notice the styling.
keyCharacteristics: Dense but readable task and project surfaces.
Restrained blue-cyan accent used for selection, focus, and primary actions.
Tinted cool neutrals for background, sidebar, card, border, and muted text.
Stable 8px-or-less corner vocabulary across panels, buttons, cards, and controls.
Motion only for state changes, such as panel width, hover, focus, and active press.

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Deadline Blue is for action, selection, focus, and completion state. Do not use it as decorative fill.

rules.name: The Small Semantic Rule
rules.body: Red, amber, and emerald should appear as status markers, badges, or small text, never as section backgrounds.

rules.name: The Product Type Rule
rules.body: Do not introduce display fonts, fluid type, or decorative letter spacing. Product surfaces use fixed sizes and a single sans stack.

rules.name: The Border First Rule
rules.body: Use borders and tonal surfaces before shadows. If a surface is static, shadow should be subtle or absent.

dos: Do keep the app task-first. Calendar, overview, projects, settings, and detail panes should expose actions immediately.
Do use Deadline Blue only for primary actions, selected states, focus, and completion affordances.
Do keep cards and controls at 8px radius or less.
Do preserve dense metadata in task rows when it supports scanning.
Do use borders and tinted panels before adding shadow.
Do keep bilingual labels short and stable, especially in buttons and narrow rails.
donts: Don't use marketing-first hero layouts that explain the app instead of letting the user work.
Don't use decorative gradient text, glassmorphism, neon dashboards, or heavy purple-blue AI-tool styling.
Don't make the productivity UI overly cute with playful mascots, oversized empty states, or motivational copy.
Don't create spreadsheet-like density without hierarchy.
Don't use non-standard controls that make common task operations feel invented or unfamiliar.
Don't use colored side-stripe borders on cards or task rows. Use full borders, status dots, or chips.
